channel.title: The Grant Desk
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in tech, art and research, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk.

item.title: Artist-in-Residence: Reimagining AI at the EU Policy Lab - deadline 22 May 2026 (27 days left)
item.description:
Joint Research Centre, European Commission

Location: Brussels, Belgium (with possible site visit in Ispra, Italy)

Award: €15,000

Duration: 6 months

Deadline: 22 May 2026 (27 days left)

Open call for an artist residency reimagining AI at the EU Policy Lab. The 15,000 EUR contracted budget covers expert fees, allowance, accommodation and travel during on-site work.

Open call details

item.title: O'Shaughnessy Fellowships - deadline 30 Apr 2026 (5 days left)
item.description:
OSV Fellowship

Location: Remote

Award: Up to $100,000

Duration: 1 year (remote)

Deadline: 30 Apr 2026 (5 days left)

Equity-free fellowship open to adults worldwide. No application fee. Includes access to investors, partners, mentors and optional in-person gatherings for Fellows.

Open call details

item.title: Open Call: SIT/PLU Residencies 2027 - deadline 31 May 2026 (36 days left)
item.description:
Baltan Laboratories

Location: Eindhoven, Netherlands

Award: TBC

Duration: TBC

Deadline: 31 May 2026 (36 days left)

Open call for SIT/PLU residencies in 2027 hosted by Baltan Laboratories. Deadline 31 May 2026, 23:59 CET. Full details and budget on the linked page.

Open call details

item.title: Making Visible the Invisible: Critical Pathways Fellowships 2026 - deadline 27 Apr 2026 (2 days left)
item.description:
Critical Pathways community and Towards a Circular Economy and Society, Utrecht University

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

Award: Up to €10,000 (travel, accommodation, living expenses, visa)

Duration: 4 to 6 weeks (Oct to Nov 2026, dates flexible)

Deadline: 27 Apr 2026 (2 days left)

Fellowship for anyone whose work uncovers hidden stories, systems and power structures behind ecological breakdown and inequality, and reimagines fairer alternatives. Open to artists, filmmakers, writers, activists, organisers, journalists, legal practitioners, policy advocates, conservationists, sustainability practitioners, entrepreneurs and academics from any discipline. No PhD required. 2026 theme: Making Visible the Invisible (overlooked consequences of how we live, produce and consume; who bears hidden costs; shifting from extractive to regenerative systems). Apply by sending the form (.docx) plus CV to criticalpathways@uu.nl. Notifications within 6 weeks of deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Critical Playground: Freelance Writer Commissions - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Critical Playground

Location: Remote

Award: Paid commissions

Duration: Per article (800 to 900 words)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Commissioned long-form writing at the intersection of design, technology, art and culture. Editorial themes: designing with AI as cultural and infrastructural system; responsive and adaptive materials; politics of platforms and creative-infrastructure governance; post-digital hybrid making; designing for collapse and continuity; creative research as practice. In-depth pieces only, no press releases or promotional copy.

Open call details

item.title: XAIxArts 4: 4th International Workshop on Explainable AI for the Arts - deadline Closed 23 Apr 2026
item.description:
ACM Creativity & Cognition (University of the Arts London)

Location: London, UK

Award: Workshop participation (funding TBC)

Duration: 1 day workshop, 13 July 2026

Deadline: Closed 23 Apr 2026

Call for papers, pictorials or videos for a workshop bringing together HCI, interaction design, AI, explainable AI and digital-arts researchers and practitioners to examine explainability through creative and artistic domains. Notifications 14 May 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Open Call: Netstalgia (Not Acceptable) - Error 406 - deadline 04 May 2026 (9 days left)
item.description:
Error 417: Expectation Fail

Location: Online

Award: €1,500 to €7,000 per project

Duration: Project-based (work delivery deadline 30 October 2026)

Deadline: 04 May 2026 (9 days left)

Open call for artists, curators and collectives worldwide to propose projects that take a clear-eyed view of internet histories and explore how they can open pathways toward more equitable futures. The brief: what protocols, networks, archives and shared conditions are needed to build the internet we actually want? Respond to netstalgia with error 406 not acceptable. Jury: Chia Amisola, Olia Lialina, Peter Sunde and Vladan Joler. Project deadline 4 May 2026 (23:59 CEST); work delivery 30 October 2026.

Open call details

item.title: The Kyoto Retreat 2026 - deadline 17 Jul 2026 (83 days left)
item.description:
The Kyoto Retreat (founded by Dexter Wimberly)

Location: Kyoto, Japan

Award: Roundtrip flight, private accommodation and $800 USD stipend

Duration: 4 weeks (1 to 30 November 2026)

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026 (83 days left)

Four-week residency in Kyoto for artists, curators and writers from anywhere in the world, working in drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, film, video, new media, installation, fiction and nonfiction writing, poetry, interdisciplinary or social practice. Open to all career stages, 21 and over. Selected participants notified by 1 September 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Impact Award for Native American Art - deadline 27 Apr 2026 (2 days left)
item.description:
Harpo Foundation

Location: USA (open to applicants worldwide)

Award: $25,000 fellowship

Duration: One-time award

Deadline: 27 Apr 2026 (2 days left)

Fellowship to amplify the contributions of under-recognised Native American contemporary visual artists.

Open call details

item.title: The Lighthouse Works Fellowship - deadline 15 Oct 2026 (173 days left)
item.description:
The Lighthouse Works (supported in part by the Robert Rauschenberg Foundation)

Location: Fishers Island, New York, USA

Award: $1,750 stipend + private bedroom, food, studio space, wood and metal fabrication shop and kiln access

Duration: 6 weeks (5 cycles per year)

Deadline: 15 Oct 2026 (173 days left)

Six-week fellowship for cultural producers at any career stage working in the vanguard of their creative fields. Five cycles run March to December. Fellows commit to an Artist Talk and Open Studio bookending the residency. 2026 application window has closed (Sep 15 to Oct 15, 2025); 2027 round expected Sep 15 to Oct 15, 2026 via Slideroom. Selection notifications mid-January.

Open call details

item.title: Writers' Colony at Dairy Hollow - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Writers' Colony at Dairy Hollow

Location: Eureka Springs, Arkansas, USA

Award: Housing, meals and lectures

Duration: Variable

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Uninterrupted residency time for writers of all genres, including culinary writers and composers. Housing and meals provided. Rolling deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Culture Moves Europe: Call for Individual Mobility 2025-2026 - deadline 30 Apr 2026 (5 days left)
item.description:
Creative Europe (Culture Moves Europe)

Location: Creative Europe countries (incl. overseas territories and outermost regions)

Award: €85/day + €400 to €800 travel + top-ups (green, family, visa, accessibility)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 30 Apr 2026 (5 days left)

Individual or small-group (up to 5 people) mobility grant for artists and cultural professionals working in architecture, cultural heritage, design and fashion, literature, music, performing arts and visual arts. Project must take place in another Creative Europe country with an international partner, pursuing 2 of: learn, create, explore, connect. No experience level required. Rolling deadlines, next on 30 April 2026.

Open call details

item.title: 2026 European Cooperation Projects (CREA-CULT-2026-COOP) - deadline 05 May 2026 (10 days left)
item.description:
Creative Europe (European Commission)

Location: Creative Europe countries

Award: Project-based grant

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 05 May 2026 (10 days left)

Transnational cooperation grant for cultural and creative organisations of all sizes (incl. micro and small) from different programme countries to co-produce, cooperate, experiment, innovate, be mobile and learn from each other. Can cover one or more cultural and creative sectors and can be interdisciplinary. Aims to broaden access to European culture and promote innovation and creativity.

Open call details

item.title: Art Commission (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Commission artists to create new work in publicly accessible spaces such as hospitals or museums. Open to individuals and organisations. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Artist Basic (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Variable

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Support for visual artists with 4+ years of experience to expand their portfolio, increase visibility or conduct research. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Artist Project (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands or abroad

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Funding for concrete artistic plans, research projects, or working periods in the Netherlands or abroad. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Artist Start (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Support for visual artists at the beginning of their careers with plans for new work. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Awareness of the History of Slavery (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline 16 Jul 2026 (82 days left)
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026 (82 days left)

Support for artistic plans addressing awareness of the history of slavery. Open to individuals and organisations.

Open call details

item.title: Curator Researcher (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Research-period based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Funding for preliminary research into exhibitions, events, articles or presentations. For curators. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Protected Cultural Heritage (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Variable

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Support for owners and managers of objects registered as protected heritage. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Voucher Artisan (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Voucher to assist hiring artisans for artistic research or work production. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Voucher Artist with Child (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Continuous support

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Voucher covering childcare costs for visual artists with dependents under compulsory school age. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Voucher Development (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Variable

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Funding for developing and deepening artistic practice. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Voucher International (Mondriaan Fund) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Outside the Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Short-term

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Quick-response funding for international programme participation outside the Netherlands. Continuous deadline.

Open call details

item.title: Karlsruhe UCCoMA: Project Funding Program for Media Art (Annual) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
City of Karlsruhe Department of Cultural Affairs / UNESCO City of Media Arts (UCCoMA)

Location: Karlsruhe, Germany (Baden-Württemberg-primary; worldwide institutions can apply)

Award: Share of €100,000 annual total volume

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Annual jury-selected call for media-art projects designed for outdoor spaces with high public visibility, addressing contemporary social issues with a sustainability focus. International exchange, collaboration and interdisciplinarity are key. Aimed at artists and cultural workers from Baden-Württemberg; open to artistic, cultural, scientific and creative-industry institutions from all over the world. Excludes school/university coursework and already-realised projects. The 2025 deadline (15 May) has passed; 2026 round dates TBC, check the page for updates.

Open call details

item.title: Karlsruhe UCCoMA: General Project Funding for Media Art - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
City of Karlsruhe Department of Cultural Affairs / UNESCO City of Media Arts (UCCoMA)

Location: Karlsruhe, Germany (Karlsruhe-resident applicants only)

Award: Project-based (smaller media-art projects); also includes networking, infrastructure, third-party funding advice and PR support

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Rolling general project funding for smaller media-art works, events and research. Covers interdisciplinary projects across music, theatre, dance, visual arts, literature, film, architecture, socioculture, intercultural and interreligious dialogue, and children's and youth culture. For artists, professionals, initiatives and institutions based in Karlsruhe (group applications must include at least one person/institution with first residence or registered office in Karlsruhe). Applied for via the City of Karlsruhe online application; selected by UCCoMA Office staff.

Open call details

item.title: Rotating AiRs: Residency on Colonialism and Climate Change - deadline 01 May 2026 (6 days left)
item.description:
Hotel Maria Kapel (HMK), La Capella, Kunsthal Extra City, MORPHO, Picha Art Centre, Cemeti Institute, AKI AORA, Casa Snowapple

Location: Mexico City / Lubumbashi / Yogyakarta (start) to Hoorn / Antwerp / Barcelona (end)

Award: €16,800 artist fee + €3,000 production budget; transport and accommodation provided

Duration: 7 months (1 month inspiration + 2 pre-production + 2 production + 2 exhibition)

Deadline: 01 May 2026 (6 days left)

Transnational residency for visual artists working at the intersection of colonialism and climate change. One month of site-specific research in Mexico City, Lubumbashi or Yogyakarta, followed by two months across three European institutions, culminating in an exhibition in Antwerp (Kunsthal Extra City), Barcelona (La Capella) or Hoorn (HMK). Online info session 8 April 2026 (15:00 CET). Interview round 9 June, final selection 1 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Zoöp Connections 2026 - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
EUNIC Netherlands

Location: Netherlands

Award: Temporary stay with housing included

Duration: Temporary residency

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Residency programme inviting practitioners to stay with Dutch Zoöps and explore how cultural institutions can learn to notice and relate differently to multispecies ecologies.

Open call details

item.title: Vila 31 x Art Explora Residency Programme, 2027 Sessions - deadline 15 May 2026 (20 days left)
item.description:
Art Explora (Vila 31, Tirana)

Location: Tirana, Albania

Award: €800/month living grant + up to €1,500 production grant + round-trip travel + 50-60m² studio-apartment (collective version: €1,200/month + €1,500 production, shared)

Duration: 3 months (Jan-Mar, May-Jul, or Sep-Nov 2027)

Deadline: 15 May 2026 (20 days left)

Three-month residency at Vila 31 in Tirana with 8 studio-apartments. Open to artists, collectives, researchers, curators and writers of all nationalities with at least 5 years of professional activity and national/international exhibition record. SOLO and COLLECTIVE tracks across visual arts, performance, digital, curatorial, writing, plus research in humanities, social sciences, cultural studies, history, technology and ecology. Up to 30 residents selected per year. No output required, but Open Studios participation is mandatory. Selection committee includes Alicia Knock (Centre Pompidou), Adela Demetja, Adam Szymczyk, Nora Razian (Art Jameel), Lucia Pietroiusti (Hartwig Art Foundation), Raphael Fonseca (Denver Art Museum). Notifications July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Open Call: Peripheral Grounds, Resonant Centers (Sound and Performative Residency) - deadline 30 Apr 2026 (5 days left)
item.description:
GRUND1535 and Positive Futures Festival

Location: Rietz, Tyrol, Austria (with festival across Innsbruck)

Award: €1,000 artist fee + accommodation + up to €600 travel + Tyrol public transport ticket

Duration: 1 to 26 October 2026

Deadline: 30 Apr 2026 (5 days left)

Short-term residency for one sound or performative artist exploring how sound, voice, language and body operate across and beyond perceived boundaries. Develop an individual project, present a live performance at Positive Futures Festival 2026, and host a public workshop, lecture or artist talk. Apply with portfolio, motivation letter and a 2-page project proposal to home@grund1535.com (subject: 'Peripheral Grounds'). Selection results by end of May 2026. Supported by State of Tyrol and City of Innsbruck.

Open call details

item.title: 2027 Elevate Prize - deadline 08 May 2026 (13 days left)
item.description:
Elevate Prize Foundation

Location: Worldwide (US 501(c)(3) or international equivalent)

Award: $300,000 unrestricted ($250,000 to organisation + $50,000 to the individual)

Duration: 2-year tailored programme + global community

Deadline: 08 May 2026 (13 days left)

Nomination-based prize for nonprofit leaders driving meaningful change. Each winner receives $300,000 in unrestricted funding plus a two-year tailored programme with visibility, scaling support and a global community. Eligibility: 18+, serving as Executive Director, CEO, Founder or equivalent of a registered nonprofit (US 501(c)(3) or international equivalent) able to receive philanthropic funds. Ideally for organisations with annual budgets between $2M and $10M (others may be considered based on readiness and impact opportunity).

Open call details

item.title: NEH Media Projects: Development and Production Grants - deadline 25 Jun 2026 (61 days left)
item.description:
National Endowment for the Humanities (NEH)

Location: United States

Award: Up to $75,000 (Development) | $350,000 (Radio/Podcast Production) | $700,000 (Documentary Production)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 25 Jun 2026 (61 days left)

Supports development, production and distribution of radio programmes, podcasts and documentary films that engage general audiences with humanities ideas. Proposals must build on sound humanities scholarship, present multiple perspectives, involve external humanities scholars at all phases, involve appropriate media professionals, use accessible formats, and show potential to attract a large public audience. Development awards (up to $75,000) cover scholar meetings, preliminary interviews, treatments and scripts, work-in-progress trailers, outreach planning and archival research.

Open call details

item.title: NGI Fediversity (11th call): Hosting Stack of the Future - deadline 01 Jun 2026 (37 days left)
item.description:
NLnet / NGI Fediversity (funded by EU Horizon Europe)

Location: Europe and beyond

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (€450,000 total budget for open calls)

Duration: Project-based (programme runs to November 2027)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026 (37 days left)

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for projects that strengthen Fediversity, an effort to bring easy-to-use hosted cloud services with service portability and personal freedom at their core to everyone. Built on NixOS for declarative, reproducible, reliable deployment of Fediverse services (PeerTube, Mastodon, Owncast, Lemmy), email, VPN, private cloud storage, wikis and more. Project results released under open source licences. Deadline 1 June 2026, 12:00 CEST.

Open call details

item.title: NGI TALER (13th call): Privacy-Preserving Digital Payments - deadline 01 Jun 2026 (37 days left)
item.description:
NLnet / NGI TALER (with GNU Taler community, funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Europe and beyond

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (€676,000 total budget for open calls)

Duration: Project-based (programme runs to November 2026)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026 (37 days left)

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for free and open source efforts aligned with privacy-preserving digital payments. Build new GNU Taler capabilities, auxiliary tools, UX, FOSS-app and open-standard integrations (P2P micropayments in messengers, social media, video conferencing), or merchant-backend improvements. All results released under a free or open-source licence. Strong projects can scale up via NGI0 Core. Deadline 1 June 2026, 12:00 CEST.

Open call details

item.title: NGI Zero Commons Fund (13th call) - deadline 01 Jun 2026 (37 days left)
item.description:
NLnet / NGI Zero (funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Europe and beyond

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (scalable for proven potential)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026 (37 days left)

Small to medium-sized R&D grants to help reclaim the public nature of the internet across the full technology spectrum: libre silicon, middleware, P2P infrastructure, end-user applications, open standards, open data and AI, open science, creative commons and open educational resources. Project results released under free or open-source licences. Aimed at troubleshooters tackling hard, important internet-commons challenges. Total programme: €21.6m through 2027. Deadline 1 June 2026, 12:00 CEST.

Open call details

item.title: Transitional Justice with Artists Grant and Fellowship - deadline 02 May 2026 (7 days left)
item.description:
Framer Framed, AFIELD and Arts of the Working Class

Location: Europe-based (residency in Amsterdam at Framer Framed)

Award: €10,000 per fellow (€9,000 unrestricted + €1,000 travel/residency)

Duration: 6 months (Cohort 1: Jul-Dec 2026, Cohort 2: May-Oct 2027)

Deadline: 02 May 2026 (7 days left)

Six grants and a 6-month fellowship for practicing artists, cultural practitioners or key members of initiatives addressing legacies of injustice and violence. Supports Europe-based practitioners and the communities they serve who have endured severe human rights violations. Includes joining AFIELD's network of 140+ socially engaged artists, a published feature in Arts of the Working Class (70,000+ print run), a 7-day residency at Framer Framed Amsterdam, and regular thematic online meetings.

Open call details

item.title: The White Pube Creatives Grant - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
The White Pube

Location: United Kingdom

Award: £500 (no strings attached)

Duration: One-off (monthly recipient)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Monthly £500 grant for a different working-class creative practitioner based in the UK. Open to anyone making stuff: art, writing, performance, sound, music, craft, comedy, games. Money can be used for time, materials, equipment, research, subscriptions, development, travel, or rent and bills. Apply by emailing funding@thewhitepube.com with a brief intro, contact and a work sample. Rolling, no deadlines, no reporting expected. Non-recipients stay in consideration for future months without re-applying.

Open call details

item.title: Working-Class Creative Grant (Karim Boumjimar) - deadline Rolling / undated
item.description:
Karim Boumjimar (independent artist initiative)

Location: Online / Remote

Award: €500 (no strings attached)

Duration: One-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Independent artist-run grant redistributing a portion of Karim Boumjimar's artwork sales as a no-strings-attached 500 EUR award for working-class creatives anywhere in the world. Funds may be used for artistic production, research, materials, travel or basic living needs. Simple application: short intro and a sample of work or interests. No fee, no reporting, no obligation to produce. Rolling review based on available funds; non-selected applicants stay in the pool for future rounds.

Open call details
